nodes.name: LASS Statement of Intent and Report
Significant uncertainty and po...
nodes.description: Significant uncertainty and potential long-term impact of COVID-19 on the tourism sector, leading to business closures and unemployment.
nodes.mitigation: Advocating for extended wage subsidies (20 weeks), implementing hibernation strategies for seasonal operators, and executing a three-tiered response plan (mitigate, restart, reimagine) focusing on domestic and intra-regional travel.

nodes.name: Drastic reduction in internati...
nodes.description: Drastic reduction in international and domestic airline connectivity, severely limiting tourism recovery timelines.
nodes.mitigation: Incremental restart strategy for domestic flights; focusing marketing efforts on the drive-market (3-hour radius) for the next 6-12 months while preparing for long-term international recovery (2-5 years).

nodes.name: Potential breach of financial ...
nodes.description: Potential breach of financial covenants by member councils due to revenue losses and increased debt loads.
nodes.mitigation: Ongoing monitoring by LGFA and DIA; leveraging government support (e.g., standby facilities, Reserve Bank bond buybacks) and prioritizing essential infrastructure investments while deferring non-essential spending.

nodes.name: Risk of resource constraints a...
nodes.description: Risk of resource constraints and staff burnout at the council level, hindering the ability to support regional collaborative projects (LASS).
nodes.mitigation: Proactive outreach to identify freed-up capacity from other councils and matching specific skill sets to project needs; prioritizing essential work and fostering regional resource sharing.

nodes.name: Increased health and safety ri...
nodes.description: Increased health and safety risks to council staff (mental and physical) due to the COVID-19 pandemic and shift to new working environments (e.g., level 3 protocols).
nodes.mitigation: Implementing specific health and safety protocols for field staff, utilizing EAP services for mental well-being, and continuous monitoring of staff locations and health status through team leaders.

nodes.name: Airport Statement of Intent and Financial Outlook
Significant and sustained redu...
nodes.description: Significant and sustained reduction in international tourism revenue and visitors, with recovery expected to take two to five years.
nodes.mitigation: Pivot to domestic tourism (drive market), implementation of a three-tiered response (mitigate, restart, reimagine), and focusing on local, regional, and event-based tourism.

nodes.name: High risk of business failures...
nodes.description: High risk of business failures in tourism and hospitality sectors post-12-week wage subsidy period.
nodes.mitigation: Lobbying the government for wage subsidy extensions to 20 weeks and facilitating workforce redeployment (e.g., placing displaced staff into the horticulture sector).

nodes.name: Potential for breach of financ...
nodes.description: Potential for breach of financial covenants for councils with high debt-to-revenue ratios during the recovery phase.
nodes.mitigation: Active monitoring through DIA modeling and working group collaboration, and leveraging long-term financing options provided by the LGFA to maintain liquidity.

nodes.name: Supply chain and staffing cons...
nodes.description: Supply chain and staffing constraints for shared services and infrastructure projects due to councils focusing internal resources on COVID-19 responses.
nodes.mitigation: Early communication with council CEOs to identify and realign specific skill sets and potential reallocation of personnel to critical regional projects.

nodes.name: Uncertainty regarding the reop...
nodes.description: Uncertainty regarding the reopening of airline connectivity, which directly impacts passenger numbers and revenue for the regional airport.
nodes.mitigation: Utilization of diversified property income, maintaining a conservative financial model that assumes limited aeronautical activity until late 2020, and securing standby facilities.

nodes.name: Mental health and well-being r...
nodes.description: Mental health and well-being risks for staff caused by the external pressures of the COVID-19 pandemic.
nodes.mitigation: Utilization of Employee Assistance Programs (EAP) and development of specific health and safety protocols to manage staff wellbeing under varying alert levels.

nodes.name: Approval of Airport Group Statements of Intent and Financials
Acceptance of Health and Safety Report
Review Jet Park restructuring and recovery plan
Financial instability and reve...
nodes.description: Financial instability and revenue loss due to COVID-19 impact on tourism and related business sectors.
nodes.mitigation: Development of a three-tiered response plan (mitigate, restart, reimagine), lobbying the government for extended wage subsidies, and focusing on domestic travel and local marketing campaigns.

nodes.name: Significant reduction in aeron...
nodes.description: Significant reduction in aeronautical income and terminal operations due to flight suspensions.
nodes.mitigation: Implementation of a pandemic plan, significant cost reductions (including payroll and director fees), utilization of government wage subsidies, and reliance on diversified property revenue/land sales.

nodes.name: Difficulty in maintaining reso...
nodes.description: Difficulty in maintaining resource support from local councils for shared services due to competing COVID-19 response priorities.
nodes.mitigation: Early proactive communication with councils to identify available staff with specific skill sets for redeployment into shared service projects.

nodes.name: Risk of council partners breac...
nodes.description: Risk of council partners breaching financial covenants due to sudden revenue decline.
nodes.mitigation: Ongoing financial modeling in conjunction with the Department of Internal Affairs and leveraging support mechanisms, such as government standby facilities and Reserve Bank bond buybacks.

nodes.name: Potential mental health and we...
nodes.description: Potential mental health and well-being risks for staff resulting from the external pressures of the COVID-19 pandemic.
nodes.mitigation: Utilizing EAP (Employee Assistance Program) services, developing specific health and safety protocols for remote and field workers, and monitoring staff health status through internal registers.

nodes.name: Loss of China group travel imp...
Adoption of Three-Tiered COVID-19 Response Plan
Implementation of Health and Safety Commitment
Financial instability and sign...
nodes.description: Financial instability and significant revenue loss across the tourism sector (accommodation, retail, hospitality) due to COVID-19.
nodes.mitigation: Advocating for extended wage subsidies (up to 20 weeks), implementing business hibernation strategies, and promoting domestic/intra-regional tourism recovery plans.

nodes.name: Major disruption and reduction...
nodes.description: Major disruption and reduction of international airline connectivity, impacting tourism and economic inflow for 2-5 years.
nodes.mitigation: Focusing on a domestic-first and trans-Tasman/Pacific travel strategy, with an incremental restart of flight schedules.

nodes.name: Loss of major events and conve...
nodes.description: Loss of major events and convention center delays creating gaps in economic activity for the Waipa district.
nodes.mitigation: Leveraging existing event spaces like Claudelands and Mystery Creek to fill gaps, shifting to virtual event formats, and postponement rather than cancellation of major events.

nodes.name: Potential strain on council st...
nodes.description: Potential strain on council staff resources and capacity to manage essential services while simultaneously contributing to cross-council shared service initiatives.
nodes.mitigation: Proactive outreach to identify available staff capacity and re-deploying personnel from non-essential areas to support regional projects.

nodes.name: Risk of breaching financial go...
nodes.description: Risk of breaching financial governance thresholds (debt-to-revenue ratios) due to COVID-19 revenue impacts.
nodes.mitigation: Close monitoring through the LGFA, utilizing existing debt facilities, and potential adjustment of capital expenditure programs to maintain fiscal health.

nodes.name: Health and safety risks to emp...
nodes.description: Health and safety risks to employees and community members due to the mental and physical impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic.
nodes.mitigation: Implementing robust protocols for remote work and safe re-entry for essential staff, utilizing EAP services for mental health, and continuous monitoring of staff well-being.
